overview: This rodent is likely a Central American agouti (Dasyprocta punctata), a species common in the forests of Central and South America. Agoutis are large rodents, often reaching the size of a house cat, and are closely related to guinea pigs. These animals play a vital role in their ecosystem by acting as "forest planters," burying seeds to store food and facilitating new tree growth. iNaturalist +5 6 sites Central American Agouti (Dasyprocta punctata) · iNaturalist Central American Agouti (Dasyprocta punctata) · iNaturalist. This rodent is likely a Central American agouti (Dasyprocta punctata), a species common in the forests of Central and South America.
Agoutis are large rodents, often reaching the size of a house cat, and are closely related to guinea pigs.
These animals play a vital role in their ecosystem by acting as "forest planters," burying seeds to store food and facilitating new tree growth.
